description_long: This object is a cylindrical black candle housed in a metal cup, with a slightly flared rim and a matte, somewhat worn surface. The front features a hand-written label bordered in red, which references Civil War camp equipment and a historical figure, suggesting it may be a commemorative or antique item. The label is affixed to the exterior and is the only decorative element, while the rest of the surface is plain and dark. The cup appears to be made of metal, likely tin or iron, and is deep enough to contain a substantial amount of wax. There is a small, subtle handle visible on one side, blending into the dark surface. The interior is not clearly visible, but the object is likely hollow and designed to hold a candle or wax.
description: A black metal cup candle with a hand-written historical label and a subtle side handle, featuring a worn, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand-written label bordered in red, with the cup's rim and part of the interior visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a small, subtle handle and the edge of the label, with the cup's dark surface prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, unadorned black surface of the cup with no visible label or handle.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the cup's smooth, dark exterior and a hint of the label on the side.
